key_findings: Total unique GT entries checked: 379 (from 614 total including year-duplicates)
Entries matched to a classification rule: 379 (100.0%)
Entries agreeing with rule: 229 (60.4% of matched)
Context-dependent cases (mostly Mfg vs SGA): 22 - these are legitimate differences where items like rent, depreciation can go to either section depending on factory vs office use
- BCIPL: 'Profit on Sale of Fixed Asset' at row 22 (II_A2) - should be non-operating income
- Depreciation: Rule sheet puts all depreciation under Item 5(vi) (manufacturing/row 55), but some CAs split between Mfg depreciation and SGA depreciation (II_C20 row 62)
- Directors Remuneration: Rule sheet maps to II_D7a (Audit Fees & Directors Remuneration, row 72), but some CAs put it under II_D1 (Salary, row 66) - both are valid SGA sub-rows

- Interest items: Rule book uses generic 'Item 9: Interest', while actual CMA form splits into term loan interest (II_F1, row 82), WC interest (II_F2, row 83), bank charges (II_F3, row 84) - CA judgment on which sub-row to use
- Non-operating income: Items like duty drawback, scrap sale can be classified as sales (II_A1) or non-operating income (II_B6a) depending on materiality and recurring nature
- Manufacturing vs SGA: Items like rent, rates & taxes, depreciation can go to either manufacturing (Item 5) or SGA (Item 6) depending on whether they relate to factory or office operations

- Unsecured loans: Rule sheet maps 'without shortfall undertaking' to Item 16 (Other term liabilities -> III_L8b), 'with shortfall undertaking' to Item 23 (Networth). GT may use III_L8a (Quasi Equity) or III_L8b (Long Term Debt) based on specific terms

- Inventory opening/closing: Rule sheet has a single rule 'Increase/Decrease in Stock' that says to bifurcate into opening and closing. GT correctly separates these into II_C12/C13a (WIP) and II_C17/C18a (FG)

Overall: The 60% agreement rate is expected given that the rule book provides generic classifications while actual CMA preparation requires company-specific judgment calls by the CA. The 22 context-dependent and 118 CA judgment cases are legitimate professional differences, not errors.
